channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Animal welfare advocates will plead with Texas lawmakers to help cities control stray pet population
item.description: One group estimated more than 568,000 cats and dogs entered shelters in 2023. About 82,000 cats and dogs were euthanized.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Berenice Garcia, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris-Walz campaign kicks off a nationwide effort to reach Native voters
item.description: Tribal leaders from across the country showed their support for the democratic ticket at Mystic Lake Event Center in Prior Lake on Wednesday. Native American civic leaders say the Harris-Walz campaign is prepared to support issues that impact Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Why Vance easily beat Walz in debate, softening his image in the process
item.description: White House hopeful JD Vance was the undisputed champion of CBS News' Vice Presidential Debate Tuesday night, according to Fox News' Howard Kurtz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The fight to recover continues for Florida farmers after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Florida farmers are facing significant challenges in their recovery following Hurricane Helene’s devastation.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kaine and Cao face off in only debate of campaign for US Senate seat from Virginia
item.description: U.S. Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia and Republican challenger Hung Cao have gone toe to toe in their only debate of the campaign over issues from illegal immigration to tariffs on foreign goods.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump calls Jack Smith filing 'pure election interference'
item.description: The former president spoke exclusively to NewsNation about the unsealing of documents related to his election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Are Nevada voters swayed by promises of 'no tax on tips'?
item.description: Reporter John Langeler from KLAS in Las Vegas joins "The Hill" to discuss whether Nevada voters are swayed by proposals to eliminate federal taxes on tips.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Watch: FOX59/CBS4 Indiana Governor Debate
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS -- For the first time this election year, the two major party candidates are having the opportunity to participate in a televised debate surrounding issues that are important for Indiana voters. U.S. Senator Mike Braun, R-Ind.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Cook County State's Attorney candidate Former Judge Eileen O’Neill Burke on crime, guns and youth violence
item.description: In deep blue Cook County, the Democratic candidate for states attorney, retired Judge Eileen O’Neill Burke, is looking past the November election and sounding like she’s already on the job.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Cybersecurity head says there's no chance a foreign adversary can change US election results
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly a month out from Election Day, the head of the nation’s cybersecurity agency is forcefully reassuring Americans who have been swept into the chaotic churn of election disinformation and distrust that they will be able to feel confident in the outcome.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris and Trump are tested by the Mideast, Helene and the port strike in the campaign's final weeks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A trio of new trials — a devastating hurricane, expanding conflict in the Mideast and a dockworkers strike that threatens the U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, ZEKE MILLER and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota abortion law signed by Walz scrutinized during VP debate
item.description: Internet searches about Minnesota’s abortion law — the first state to codify access after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade — were high during Tuesday night’s vice presidential debate.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: State of Democra-Z: Takeaways from the vice presidential debate
item.description: The vice presidential debate is not expected to push many voters to one side or another, but it offered a closer look at the men who could be second in command. We check in with two Gen-Z voters about what stood out to them.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Voters react to Vance 'non-answer' when asked if Trump won 2020 election
item.description: GOP vice presidential candidate JD Vance was questioned about former President Trump's prior statements on the 2020 elections – here's how voters reacted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Soomaalida St. Cloud waxay rabaan in ay ka dhex muuqdaan siyaasadda, laakiin iyaga oo dhan isku aragti ma aha
item.description: In ka badan labaatameeyo sano ka hor ayey qaxootigan oo ka soo jeeda Bariga Afrika soo dageen St. Cloud, oo qaarkood ayaa raadinaya in ay door ku yeeshaan maamulka iyo siyaasadda.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Biden, Harris inspect damage in hurricane-ravaged Southeast in wake of Trump visit
item.description: President Biden and Vice President Harris each travel to the storm-ravaged Southeast on Wednesday, two days after Donald Trump visited – as the death toll and devastation from Hurricane Helene soars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania town grapples with Trump assassination attempt ahead of his return
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Donald Trump is going back to Butler, where the world last saw him pump his fist and beseech followers to “fight,” even as blood streaked his face from a would-be assassin's bullet.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Trio of crises looms over the campaign's final stretch
item.description: In a debate that evoked a calmer era in American politics, Tim Walz and JD Vance went after each other’s running mates Tuesday and sought to shore up their campaigns’ vulnerabilities at a time of renewed fears of a regional war in the Middle East and sadness over devastation from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NPR fact checked the Vance-Walz vice presidential debate. Here's what we found
item.description: JD Vance and Tim Walz debated on Tuesday in the last scheduled debate of the election. The sparring was mostly collegial but a number of points on key issues require additional context or corrections.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Harris will tour Helene devastation in Georgia, North Carolina as storm scrambles campaign schedule
item.description: The Democratic nominee plans to visit Georgia on Wednesday and survey the devastation in North Carolina in the coming days, according to aides.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vance and Walz are both veterans. Here's what it means to have a vet VP
item.description: For the first time since 1996, both major parties have picked a veteran for vice president — a fact that some veterans' groups are hoping it leads to greater understanding of military and vet issues.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence, MPR News

item.title: St. Cloud Somali community seeks political influence, but not all share same views
item.description: More than two decades since the first East African refugees settled in St. Cloud, some community members are seeking a more active role in government and politics. But their political views are varied.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Walz, Vance split on abortion, border, economic policies in issues-driven debate
item.description: Questions around climate change, border concerns, abortion and the economy drove Tuesday night’s vice presidential faceoff. Vance and Walz remained largely cordial in the debate and steered away from personal attacks.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: As debate rages over coverage, rural Minnesotans face challenges getting fertility care
item.description: For many rural Minnesotans getting treatments is still really hard and expensive. The stark reality is 1 in 6 in the state are currently experiencing infertility, and for those living outside the metro, treatment options are especially limited.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: What to know about the Walz-Vance debate
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) and Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) are set to go head-to-head on Tuesday night in their first and likely only debate as vice presidential contenders.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Austrian far-right party wins national vote but its chances of governing are unclear
item.description: The Freedom Party on Sunday secured the first far-right national parliamentary election victory in post-World War II Austria. But political rivals have said they won’t work with the party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harris trolls Trump at Vegas rally and LA fundraiser, says her crowds are 'pretty big'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris poked at Donald Trump over crowd sizes, his refusal to debate again and his privileged background on Sunday as she hauled in campaign cash in California and held a raucous rally at the same Nevada venue where the Republican nominee had appeared just two
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon senator introduces bill to reform Supreme Court, add 6 more seats
item.description: Oregon Sen. Ron Wyden introduced bill on Thursday that would bring fundamental changes to the nation's high court.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, Fox 5
